edges.description: Collagen-rich fibrous connective-tissue accumulation produces the defining gingival fibromatosis lesion.
edges.source: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Fibrotic expansion of gingival connective tissue causes progressive gingival enlargement.
edges.source: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Expanding fibrous gingiva can cover tooth crowns and mechanically displace teeth.
intermediate_mechanisms: fibrous gingival overgrowth covering tooth crowns
edges.source: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Gingival overgrowth can mechanically impede eruption and prolong retention of primary dentition.
intermediate_mechanisms: fibrous gingival overgrowth covering tooth crowns
edges.source: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Severe fibrous gingival enlargement can deform the palate and impair swallowing and speech.
intermediate_mechanisms: palatal deformity from severe gingival overgrowth
edges.source: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Constitutive SOS1/RAS-MAPK and PI3K/AKT activation drives fibroblast proliferation and a profibrotic phenotype, directly fueling collagen overproduction and ECM accumulation in gingival tissue.
edges.source: Constitutive RAS-MAPK Signaling in Gingival Fibroblasts
edges.target: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: TGF-beta1/CTGF signaling and the TIMP-1/MMP-1 imbalance amplify collagen synthesis while reducing matrix degradation, converging on net fibrotic accumulation in gingival fibroblasts.
edges.source: Profibrotic Cytokine Signaling and Matrix-Remodeling Imbalance
edges.target: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: Oxidative stress increases collagen synthesis in gingival fibroblasts, while EMT expands the local fibroblast pool, together amplifying the fibrotic output.
edges.target: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

edges.description: miR-335-3p directly represses SOS1; its loss derepresses the RAS-MAPK axis, amplifying constitutive RAS activation in gingival fibroblasts.
edges.source: miR-335-3p Loss and Convergent Profibrotic Network Derepression
edges.target: Constitutive RAS-MAPK Signaling in Gingival Fibroblasts

edges.description: miR-335-3p directly targets SMAD2/3 (TGF-beta downstream effectors); its loss derepresses TGF-beta/CTGF profibrotic cytokine signaling.
edges.source: miR-335-3p Loss and Convergent Profibrotic Network Derepression
edges.target: Profibrotic Cytokine Signaling and Matrix-Remodeling Imbalance

nodes.description: The HGF1 SOS1 frameshift removes the C-terminal autoinhibitory proline-rich SH3-binding domains of the SOS1 guanine-nucleotide exchange factor, yielding a truncated protein that drives constitutive RAS activation and downstream MAPK signaling, promoting fibroblast proliferation and a profibrotic phenotype. A KCNQ1-driven feedback amplifies Ras clustering/activation and MAPK/AP-1 output in HGF fibroblasts.
nodes.id: Constitutive RAS-MAPK Signaling in Gingival Fibroblasts

evidence.snippet: introduces a frameshift and creates a premature stop codon, abolishing four functionally important proline-rich SH3 binding domains normally present in the carboxyl-terminal region of the SOS1 protein
ML277 generated lateral clustering and activation of Ras on plasma membrane, followed by augmented MAPK/AP-1 signaling pathway output.
proliferation, migration, and fibrosis response via the PI3K/AKT/mTOR and Ras/Raf/MEK/ERK pathways

nodes.description: Mechanical impedance of tooth eruption by the fibrous gingival mass, with prolonged retention of primary dentition.

evidence.snippet: the overgrowth can cover entire crowns of the teeth, thus resulting in prolonged retention of primary dentition

nodes.description: Severe gingival overgrowth can impair mastication and phonation, sometimes with palatal deformity extending to the midline.

evidence.snippet: causing deformity of the palate and impairing phonation and deglutition, even reaching the midline

nodes.description: The central lesion of HGF is overproduction and accumulation of type I collagen and other extracellular matrix macromolecules by hyperactive gingival fibroblasts, producing dense, relatively acellular fibrous connective tissue. Affected gingiva shows increased fibroblast numbers and increased collagen content, and HGF fibroblasts proliferate faster in culture.
nodes.id: Gingival Fibroblast Hyperactivity and Collagen Overproduction

evidence.snippet: Histological assessment of HGF gingiva indicated increased numbers of fibroblasts (30%) and increased collagen (10%). Cell proliferation studies demonstrated increased growth rates
The functional study supports a biological role of ZNF862 for increasing the profibrotic factors particularly COL1A1 synthesis and hence resulting in HGF.

nodes.description: Generalized or localized fibrous overgrowth of the maxillary and mandibular gingivae, the defining feature of HGF.

evidence.snippet: characterized by slow but progressive fibrous, non-hemorrhagic, and painless growth of the gingival tissues due to the increased deposition of collagen

nodes.description: Enlargement of gingival tissue that can cover the crowns of teeth and deform the dental arch.

evidence.snippet: a disorder characterized by progressive enlargement of the gingiva. This enlargement results from an increase in the connective tissue elements of the submucosa

nodes.description: HGF gingival fibroblasts exhibit metabolic alterations including increased lipid peroxidation and reduced antioxidant CoQ10, and oxidant exposure increases collagen production in vitro. Histology shows basal lamina disruption with epithelial cells migrating into connective tissue, consistent with an epithelial-mesenchymal-transition contribution to the fibroblast pool.

evidence.snippet: The results of the biochemical analysis showed increased collagen synthesis, reduced antioxidant CoQ10 content, and high levels of lipid peroxidation.
A histological study revealed dense fibrous tissue, basal lamina disruption, and epithelial cell migration into the connective tissue.

nodes.description: HGF gingival fibroblasts show elevated TGF-beta1 and connective tissue growth factor (CTGF) signaling and increased HSP47 (a collagen-specific chaperone), together with a shift in the TIMP-1/MMP-1 ratio toward reduced matrix degradation. The combination of increased collagen synthesis and decreased matrix turnover drives net fibrotic accumulation.
nodes.id: Profibrotic Cytokine Signaling and Matrix-Remodeling Imbalance

biological_processes: Transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.snippet: The synthesis of collagen I, HSP47, TGF-β1, CTGF and TIMP-1 was significantly elevated in HGF gingival fibroblasts compared with controls, while the production of MMP-1 was decreased.

nodes.description: Displacement and malposition of teeth, including diastemas and crowding, resulting from the expanding fibrous gingival mass.

evidence.snippet: the overgrowth can cover entire crowns of the teeth, thus resulting in prolonged retention of primary dentition, diastemas or malposition of teeth

nodes.description: miR-335-3p is downregulated in HGF gingival fibroblasts and in TGF-beta- stimulated normal gingival fibroblasts. This microRNA directly targets and represses several core profibrotic hubs - SOS1 (the HGF1/RAS-MAPK driver), SMAD2/3 (TGF-beta signaling), and CTNNB1 (Wnt/beta-catenin) - so its loss derepresses the convergent profibrotic program. Restoring miR-335-3p attenuates, and knocking it down promotes, the fibrogenic activity of human gingival fibroblasts, nominating it as a candidate antifibrotic target.
nodes.id: miR-335-3p Loss and Convergent Profibrotic Network Derepression

biological_processes: Transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.snippet: miR-335-3p directly targeted SOS1, SMAD2/3, and CTNNB1 by canonical and noncanonical base paring
Ectopic miR-335-3p attenuated, whereas knockdown of miR-335-3p promoted, the fibrogenic activity of human gingival fibroblasts
